name: José Fajardo
abstract: José Antonio Fajardo Ramos (October 18, 1919 – December 11, 2001)  was a Cuban charanga bandleader and flautist, who played the traditional five-keyed wooden flute.

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Palmieri
about_quotes.evidence: After him, then you have players like José Fajardo, Orquesta Aragón, Orquesta Sensación. These are all charangas.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Palmieri — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wilson "Chembo" Corniel
about_quotes.evidence: Jose Fajardo, flute. They played the black, Cuban flute, the wooden flute, five holes, open holed, open holed.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wilson "Chembo" Corniel interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, November 15, 2019
